search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae dysuria urinary symptoms fever flank pain haematuria pregnancy
site:nice.org.uk CG97 lower urinary tract symptoms initial assessment retention
site:nice.org.uk NG123 urgency frequency urine testing diary
rejection_reasons: Medication selection and doses were outside scope.
Symptom checkers are not exact clinical evidence.
Male and female sources require explicit population limits.
population_applicability: Partial: the DHA source covers dysuria with qualifiers, NICE CG97 covers adult men, and NICE NG123 covers adult women; the broad workflow has no sex or age restriction.
setting_applicability: Direct for selected DHA telehealth history and warning prompts; NICE assessment fields require local pathway adaptation.
UAE_applicability: DHA dysuria evidence is UAE-direct; NICE LUTS evidence is supporting only.
recency_verification: Current DHA Issue 2 and current NICE CG97/NG123 pages were reviewed on 2026-07-13.
superseded_check: No newer DHA issue was identified; NICE CG97 and NG123 remain current.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports selected urinary symptom and context history.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports onset, persistence, severity, duration, location, fever, rigors, nausea, vomiting, flank pain, medicine or procedure context, pregnancy and sexual context, trauma or irritants, haematuria, frequency, urgency, and voiding symptoms.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports fever, flank pain, haematuria, pregnancy, recurrence, retention, and systemic concern as prompts only.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports fever with flank pain or tenderness, vomiting, visible haematuria, pregnancy, diabetes, immunosuppression, and recurrence as prompts only, without automatic urgency or referral output.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports clinician-ordered urinalysis and qualified culture documentation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports urinalysis, recommendation-qualified urine culture, and renal-function or electrolyte review in qualified pyelonephritis, nausea, or vomiting contexts.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports broad LUTS identity and selected male assessment fields.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports symptom and medicine history, abdominal and genital examination, digital rectal examination, frequency-volume context, urinalysis, qualified PSA discussion, qualified creatinine, conservative-advice documentation, and referral criteria.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports retention and bladder-distension context for adult men only.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports acute or chronic urinary-retention context, palpable or percussable bladder, residual volume, creatinine, qualified imaging, and clinician-led follow-up or referral; catheter and medicine instructions are not mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Supports urine testing and storage-symptom assessment in adult women only.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports urine dipstick, qualified culture, post-void residual, and bladder-diary documentation in adult women.

unresolved_source_gaps: The broad triage-style complaint spans infection, storage, obstruction, pregnancy, male, recurrent, and systemic contexts and is not a diagnosis.
Hydration advice, fixed follow-up timing, diagnosis, treatment, medicine, and referral decisions remain unsupported.
